---
title: "phase1"
output: pdf_document
date: "2026-05-05"
---
```{r}
# install.packages("readxl")
# install.packages("ggplot2")
# install.packages("dplyr")
# install.packages("car")
library(readxl)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(car)
```

```{r}
# Set working directory if needed
# setwd("your/path/here")

# Read Excel file
temp <- read.csv("temp.csv")
ph <- read.csv("ph.csv")
temp_data <- temp[0:4]
ph_data <- ph[0:4]

# Ensure 'trial' and the treatment variables are factors for ANOVA
temp_data$Trial <- as.factor(temp_data$Trial)
temp_data$Temp  <- as.factor(temp_data$Temp)

ph_data$Trial <- as.factor(ph_data$Trial)
ph_data$pH    <- as.factor(ph_data$pH)

names(temp_data)[names(temp_data) == "Diameter..cm."] <- "Diameter"
names(ph_data)[names(ph_data) == "Diameter..cm."] <- "Diameter"

head(temp_data)
head(ph_data)
```

```{r}
# For Temperature Data: Clear Mean + 95% CI Plot broken down by Trial panels
ggplot(temp_data, aes(x = Temp, y = Diameter, color = Temp)) +
  stat_summary(fun.data = mean_cl_normal, geom = "errorbar", width = 0.2, size = 1) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point", size = 3.5, shape = 18) +
  facet_grid(. ~ Trial, labeller = label_both) +
  labs(
    title = "Mean Fungal Growth (95% CI) Across Temperature Treatments",
    subtitle = "Separated by Experimental Trial to view distribution consistency",
    x = "Temperature Level",
    y = "Colony Diameter (cm)"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(legend.position = "none") # Legend hidden because x-axis already displays temperature labels

# For pH Data: Clear Mean + 95% CI Plot broken down by Trial panels
ggplot(ph_data, aes(x = pH, y = Diameter, color = pH)) +
  stat_summary(fun.data = mean_cl_normal, geom = "errorbar", width = 0.2, size = 1) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point", size = 3.5, shape = 18) +
  facet_grid(. ~ Trial, labeller = label_both) +
  labs(
    title = "Mean Fungal Growth (95% CI) Across pH Treatments",
    subtitle = "Separated by Experimental Trial to highlight interaction changes",
    x = "pH Level",
    y = "Colony Diameter (cm)"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
```
```{r}
# Temperature Interaction Plot
ggplot(temp_data, aes(x = as.numeric(as.character(Temp)), y = Diameter, color = Trial, group = Trial)) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "line", size = 1) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point", size = 3) +
  labs(title = "Interaction Plot: Mean Fungal Growth across Temperatures",
       x = "Temperature",
       y = "Mean Diameter (cm)") +
  theme_classic()

# pH Interaction Plot
ggplot(ph_data, aes(x = as.numeric(as.character(pH)), y = Diameter, color = Trial, group = Trial)) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "line", size = 1) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point", size = 3) +
  labs(title = "Interaction Plot: Mean Fungal Growth across pH Levels",
       x = "pH Level",
       y = "Mean Diameter (cm)") +
  theme_classic()
```


```{r temp-model}
# 1. Ensure Temp is numeric for polynomial regression modeling
temp_data_reg <- temp_data
temp_data_reg$Temp <- as.numeric(as.character(temp_data_reg$Temp))

# 2. Fit basic quadratic model (No Trial factor included)
temp_model_final <- lm(Diameter ~ Temp + I(Temp^2), data = temp_data_reg)

# 3. Print evaluation outputs
cat("\n--- Temperature Model Summary ---\n")
print(summary(temp_model_final))

cat("\n--- ANOVA Table for Temperature Regression ---\n")
print(anova(temp_model_final))

# 4. Check assumptions
plot(temp_model_final, which = 1, main = "Residuals vs Fitted: Temperature Model")
plot(temp_model_final, which = 2, main = "Normal Q-Q: Temperature Model")

```

```{r}
bc <- boxcox(Diameter+0.01 ~ Temp + I(Temp^2), data = temp_data_reg)
lambda <- bc$x[which.max(bc$y)]
print(paste("The optimal lambda is:", round(lambda, 2)))


temp_data_reg$Transformed_Diameter <- (temp_data_reg$Diameter + 0.01)^0.38

# 3. Fit the quadratic model on the transformed data (No Trial factor needed)
temp_bc_model <- lm(Transformed_Diameter ~ Temp + I(Temp^2), data = temp_data_reg)

# 4. Print evaluation outputs
cat("\n--- Box-Cox Transformed Temperature Model Summary (\u03bb = 0.38) ---\n")
print(summary(temp_bc_model))

cat("\n--- ANOVA Table for Box-Cox Regression ---\n")
print(anova(temp_bc_model))

# 5. Check updated assumptions to see if funnel shape and Q-Q tails improved
plot(temp_bc_model, which = 1, main = "Residuals vs Fitted: Box-Cox Model")
plot(temp_bc_model, which = 2, main = "Normal Q-Q: Box-Cox Model")
```


```{r ph-model}
# 1. Ensure pH is numeric for regression, but keep Trial as a Factor
ph_data_reg <- ph_data
ph_data_reg$pH    <- as.numeric(as.character(ph_data_reg$pH))
ph_data_reg$Trial <- as.factor(ph_data_reg$Trial)

# 2. Fit specific model: Linear interaction with Trial + Global Quadratic term
ph_model_final <- lm(Diameter ~ pH:Trial + I(pH^2), data = ph_data_reg)

# 3. Print evaluation outputs
cat("\n--- pH Model Summary ---\n")
print(summary(ph_model_final))

cat("\n--- ANOVA Table for pH Regression ---\n")
print(anova(ph_model_final))

# 4. Check assumptions
plot(ph_model_final, which = 1, main = "Residuals vs Fitted: pH Model")
plot(ph_model_final, which = 2, main = "Normal Q-Q: pH Model")
```
